tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine and low total CO₂, suggesting impaired renal function and possible metabolic acidosis. Marked proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) further support significant renal pathology, possibly nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy. Elevated alkaline phosphatase may indicate hepatic or bone involvement. Prompt nephrology evaluation is recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
